title: /inspector/v1/replay-drive mutation endpoint (KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1 envelope)
goal: Per spec §6.5.4 (AC-INSPECTOR-PLANE-MUTATION-ROUTING) implement the sole mutation endpoint `/inspector/v1/replay-drive` that accepts ONLY a catalog action id plus a signed `WriteBoxV1` envelope; any other body returns 403; the inspector never has a parallel mutation path. The endpoint routes mutations through KernelActionCatalogV1 (shipped by KERNEL-002).
expected_diff_shape: POST handler added to inspector_server.rs (MT-029); RequestEnvelope deserialization + signature verification; tests for accepted/rejected envelopes and KernelActionCatalogV1 routing.
unblock_conditions: MT-029 server scaffold ships; KernelActionCatalogV1 exists in main from KERNEL-002 (verify via cargo doc or Grep before authoring).
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.5.4 the endpoint surface is STRICTLY: POST /inspector/v1/replay-drive with body `{ action_id: String, envelope: WriteBoxV1Envelope }`. WriteBoxV1Envelope is the signed envelope type from KERNEL-002 (refer to src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/write_box/* for the actual type — confirm via Grep). The handler: (1) deserialize body; reject malformed with 400; (2) verify envelope signature via KERNEL-002's WriteBox verifier; reject invalid with 403; (3) look up action_id in KernelActionCatalogV1; reject unknown action with 404; (4) dispatch to KernelActionCatalogV1's action handler passing the verified envelope; (5) return the action's typed response. Any body shape OTHER than `{ action_id, envelope }` returns 403 (NOT 400 — 403 is the specified status because the request was syntactically valid but the request shape itself is forbidden as a parallel-mutation attempt). This is critical: a request like `{ catalog_action: "x", payload: {...} }` (any field name other than action_id+envelope) returns 403. Note: even though MT-029 routes only GET on /inspector/v1/*, this MT adds the one POST exception explicitly. Per HBR-INT-007 the action dispatch emits an EventLedger row `INSPECTOR_REPLAY_DRIVE { action_id, envelope_signer, result }`. Tests: (a) valid envelope + known action_id -> 200 + KernelActionCatalogV1 dispatch confirmed (use a mock catalog); (b) invalid signature -> 403; (c) unknown action_id -> 404; (d) extra body field -> 403; (e) missing field -> 403.
risk_if_missed: Without strict envelope shape enforcement, the inspector becomes a parallel mutation path bypassing KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1 — spec §6.5 invariant breached.
validator_focus: ALL non-{action_id,envelope} body shapes return 403 (not 400); signature verification uses KERNEL-002 verifier (not a duplicated implementation); EventLedger row emitted on dispatch.

minimum_controls: Extra-field test asserts 403 (proves no silently-ignored fields).
Signature verification uses KERNEL-002 verifier (no duplicated crypto).
EventLedger row emitted on dispatch for post-hoc audit.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Operator stance treats any path that bypasses WriteBoxV1 as CRITICAL — the WriteBoxV1 envelope 'signature' is a keyless SHA256 of the canonical envelope body, so any local process can forge a passing replay-drive envelope under any signer; the gate that should be the sole inspector mutation surface does not authenticate at all.
